name: Converge activity-ledger to derive-on-read + remove dead schema (MMT-ADR-0022 arch E)
description: Per the MMT-ADR-0022 re-vet (WI-752), the target architecture is E: moments are derived on read; the table is seen-state + genuinely-non-reconstructable capture only. (1) Prune the 5 declared-but-unwritten LedgerKinds (topic_mastered, retention_due, needs_deepening_added, recap_ready, snapshot_ready) from @eduagent/schemas — they imply the materialize-everything model E rejects; the /now feed already derives retention on read and topic-mastery lives on the session/assessment records. (2) Establish derive-on-read as the documented default for new/derivable moments (read-time projection in now-feed.ts, not a new writer). (3) Decide whether session_filed (the one materialized kind today) moves to derive-on-read — collapsing the table toward pure seen-state — or stays as the single genuinely-materialized moment; record the call. Keep best-effort writes for whatever residual materialized surface remains.
acceptanceCriteria:
Unwritten LedgerKinds pruned from @eduagent/schemas; a documented rule that new moments derive-on-read; the session_filed materialize-vs-derive decision recorded and implemented; /now feed user-facing behavior unchanged; typecheck + related tests green.

--- Dedup: absorbs WI-766 dead activity-ledger schema removal (2026-06-24) ---
Same MMT-ADR-0022 architecture-E convergence. In addition to derive-on-read (above), this item now includes removing the dead schema (WI-766, Related):
- Drop the `visibility` column + `ledger_visibility` enum (self-only; enforced by profile scope + RLS) and the `template_key` column (copy resolved at read time; /now re-derives) via a committed migration.
- Migration MUST include a Rollback note (drops columns + a type) per Schema And Deploy Safety, and be applied before code assuming the columns are gone.
- Update drizzle schema, @eduagent/schemas, writeActivityMoment(), the auto-file-session writer, and tests; the /now reader (sole consumer, uses neither column) is unaffected; api typecheck + related tests green.
